parsed_review.summary:
CP00-247 (RP07 Search OCR And Index, risk class C, 150 units, range RP07.P02.M08.S19–RP07.P03.M05.S22) is a clean descriptor-only closeout pack whose production_ready claim is correctly conservative and fully supported by implementation, tests, and local/Hermes validation. Verification performed read-only (Read/Grep/Glob; no Bash, no writes, no connectors).

Findings supporting PASS:
1. production_ready boundary is honest: manifest.json and command-evidence.json carry production_ready=false with status "implemented_pending_review"/"implementation_and_plan_gates_passed_pending_final_closeout". The only positive gate is production_ready_candidate, which validators.js (createSearchCoreCp247HermesEvidencePacket) computes as coverage.valid && foundation.valid — a CANDIDATE, never an unconditional production_ready=true. No production_ready gate is weakened.
2. Descriptor-only / no-runtime / no-real-data boundaries hold across source, validators, tests, and manifest: freezeCp247Result and the descriptor validators stamp synthetic_only, no_real_data, writes_product_state=false, runtime_execution=false, plus ~30 no-runtime/no-leak flags and omits_unauthorized_data=true. No product runtime is embedded.
3. CP178+ review hardening present: registry.js forbidden_review_evidence enumerates failed_authentication_run, zero_byte_output, malformed_json_output, fenced_json_output, tool_call_shaped_output, disabled_tools_run, missing_raw_output_reference, missing_source_inspection_basis, claude_final_approval_claim, source_mutation_claim, production_enterprise_trust_claim — covering every required rejection case. readiness-report.json shows a clean auth preflight (exit_code 0, stdout_bytes 1434, valid JSON {"ok":true}); validation-report.json confirms forbidden_tools_present=false and summary_only_review_allowed=false honored.
4. Claude remains read-only and non-final: descriptor authority_boundary sets claude_is_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true; Claude packets expose read_only=true, required_model "claude-opus-4-8", and questions only (no approval field). NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION keeps promotes_claude_to_final_approval, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation, schema_drift_detected, and breaking_change_detected all false.
5. Hermes used only for deterministic evidence packets (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false); not embedded in product runtime.
6. Distributions reconcile exactly to 150 and match the prompt baseline (phases RP07.P02=46 / RP07.P03=104; deliverable, micro-title, and per-section counts all sum to 150); handoff targets CP00-248 / RP07.P03.M06.S01 are consistent across registry/service/validators/contract/plan; unrelated dirty/untracked files were correctly excluded from pack scope.

Remaining gates are expected, not defects: the post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) and the post-review validation command suite (npm test, npm run validate, goal/control-plane/fullplan validates, git diff --check, etc.) run after this review, and human final approval is still required to close the enterprise trust claim. This review is the read-only Claude (C07) input to that chain and does not itself close out the pack or the goal. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings.
